dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9321 IH: To temporarily expand the authorized uses of grants awarded under the Veterans Legacy Program of the National Cemetery Administration, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Nunn of Iowa (for himself and Mr. Davis of North Carolina) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To temporarily expand the authorized uses of grants awarded under the Veterans Legacy Program of the National Cemetery Administration, and for other purposes.

section.header: Veterans Legacy Program grants for locating, identifying, and interring unclaimed or abandoned remains of veterans

subsection.text: Notwithstanding section 1(a)(3) of Public Law 116–107 (38 U.S.C. 2400 note), during the period specified in subsection (b), an entity in receipt of a grant under the Veterans Legacy Program may use such grant to assist the Secretary of Veterans Affairs in—

paragraph.text: locating unclaimed or abandoned human remains suspected to be the remains of a veteran;
determining whether such remains are the remains of a veteran; or
interring such remains in a national cemetery under the jurisdiction of the Secretary.

subsection.text: The period specified in this subsection is the period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act and ending on the last day of the second fiscal year beginning after such date.

Amounts made available for grants used for the purposes described in subsection (a) shall not be requested in addition to amounts otherwise intended to be requested for the Veterans Legacy Program in any annual budget request of the President that is submitted under section 1105 of title 31, United States Code.
In this section, the term Veterans Legacy Program has the meaning given such term in Public Law 116–107.
